content.problemStatement: Write a program for the coding challenge "Find Min/Max in BST". Refer to LeetCode description: N/A
constraints: 1 <= Number of nodes in the BST <= 10^4
The input BST is a valid binary search tree
content.inputFormat: The input will be the root of a binary search tree (BST). Each node in the BST will have a unique integer value.
content.outputFormat: The output will be an object with two properties: 'min' and 'max', representing the minimum and maximum values in the BST, respectively.

solution.spaceComplexity: O(H) where H is the height of the tree

code.Python3:
class Node:
            def __init__(self, val):
               self.val = val
               self.left = None
               self.right = None

         def find_min_and_max(root):
            if root is None:
               return None, None

            min_val = root.val
            max_val = root.val

            left_min, left_max = find_min_and_max(root.left)
            right_min, right_max = find_min_and_max(root.right)

            if left_min is not None and left_min < min_val:
               min_val = left_min
            if right_min is not None and right_min < min_val:
               min_val = right_min

            if left_max is not None and left_max > max_val:
               max_val = left_max
            if right_max is not None and right_max > max_val:
               max_val = right_max

            return min_val, max_val

         root = Node(8)
         root.left = Node(3)
         root.right = Node(10)

         root.left.left = Node(1)
         root.left.right = Node(6)

         root.right.right = Node(14)

         min_val, max_val = find_min_and_max(root)

         print(f"Min: {min_val}, Max: {max_val}")
